what_it_does: A standalone specimen/deliverable page (a @dsCard 'Colors' card) that documents and demonstrates the ConceptV surface-zone language: a masthead manifesto, a JS-generated zone registry (10 tonal zones), a component-roles gallery (buttons/chips/Vi pills/badges), and an applied 're-skinned' Overview dashboard mock with a spatial path-line layer. Includes a live Tweaks panel (React/Babel) that drives intensity/palette/spatial knobs via CSS vars.
observations: Links colors_and_type.css + tonal-zoning.css; declares the page via an HTML comment @dsCard (group/name/subtitle/viewport) for the gallery harness

Masthead states the doctrine: differentiation via subtle undertones not colour blocks; ~2% undertone per nesting level = containment depth read without borders; gold reserved for Vi/active decisions/selected states; 'felt more than seen'

Plain JS builds the zone registry from a TZ_ZONES array (base, content, panel, review, source, package, success, warning, reject, vi) each with role/name/pigment token/percent/desc, swapping vi to --vi-* and --accent-gold tokens
Component roles section demos cvx-btn (primary gold / secondary / reject), cvx-chip (ready/source/pending), cvx-vi (gem) — 'only one thing is gold-filled and clickable: the primary decision'

Applied Overview mock: 3-column dashboard (nav / main / Vi rail) with zoned blocks, an SVG spatial connective layer, ghosted off-frame neighbours, and a JS buildFlow() that draws dashed path-lines through the zone-label markers
A fit() routine scales the fixed 1240px Overview design to its frame via transform:scale and a ResizeObserver

Tweaks panel (script type=text/babel, uses TweaksPanel/useTweaks from tweaks-panel.jsx) writes --zone-intensity, --gold-intensity, --spatial-opacity and palette body classes; defaults wrapped in EDITMODE-BEGIN/END markers
standouts: Articulates the core zoning principle: zoning = containment depth made visible via stacked low-% warm undertones, no cool greys, gold strictly reserved

Live edit-mode knobs (tint/gold intensity, warm/cooler/warmer palette, spatial layer) that re-tune the whole language by moving single CSS vars — demonstrates the 'one knob re-tunes everything' claim in situ
The spatial layer (dashed path-lines + diamond nodes + ghosted neighbour frames) makes 'how work moves through the system' visible, gated by --spatial-opacity
EDITMODE-BEGIN/END comment fences around TWEAK_DEFAULTS suggest a tooling round-trip that rewrites defaults
conventions: Consumes canonical tokens only ('nothing here invents colour'); zone surfaces/strong/edge/ink referenced as var(--zone-<key>-*)
@dsCard comment registration for the gallery; data-driven specimen build (TZ_ZONES / legendZones arrays) rather than hand-marked-up cards
Follows the gold-for-Vi-only rule and the felt-not-seen low-opacity intent throughout
concerns: Pins React/ReactDOM/Babel from unpkg.com CDNs with SRI — a network/external dependency and runtime Babel transform (standalone, dev builds) in what is otherwise a token specimen

ReactDOM.createRoot mounts a Tweaks app at top level — a self-mounting pattern the project's own CLAUDE.md warns can poison a shared bundle if not DOM-guarded (here it's a standalone page, so lower risk, but the pattern is present)
DESIGN_W constant is 1240 in JS while .ov width is also 1240 but the @dsCard viewport is 1360x900 — multiple width sources to keep aligned
Numerous inline styles on the Overview mock (--zone-* overrides per tile) — legitimate for a specimen but dense
notes: The flagship specimen for the tonal-zoning language: a manifesto + JS-built zone registry + component-role gallery + a live-tweakable re-skinned Overview dashboard showing depth-as-undertone, gold-for-Vi-only, and a spatial flow layer.
questions: What consumes the EDITMODE-BEGIN/END fenced TWEAK_DEFAULTS — is there an external editor that writes back into this file?
Is the unpkg CDN + runtime Babel acceptable for a deliverable, or is this expected to be bundled before shipping?
Does the JS-listed pigment/percent metadata (TZ_ZONES) stay in sync with the actual token definitions in colors_and_type.css, or could it drift?
purpose_vs_actual: Match — it presents itself as the 'Surface-Zone Language' registry + component roles + applied Overview re-skin, and it delivers all three plus a live tuning panel.
apparent_themes: Containment depth made visible through subtle warm undertones
Reserved-meaning colour (gold = Vi / decision / selection only)
Spatial connective storytelling (how work flows through zones)
Live single-knob re-tuning of the whole language
intention_signals: Trying to teach and prove the tonal-zoning system to the eye — show that structure can be read from undertone alone, that gold means one thing, and that the entire language re-tunes from single tokens — by rendering the rules as a registry plus a real applied dashboard with interactive knobs.
novelty: The interactive edit-mode panel that re-tunes the live language via CSS vars, plus the spatial path-line + ghosted-neighbour layer that visualises work-flow through zones, all in a self-contained specimen.
contribution: Contributes the canonical visual demonstration of the surface-zone language — the zone registry, the component-role vocabulary (buttons/chips/Vi pills), and a fully zoned applied Overview with a spatial flow layer and live tuning knobs that exercise the tokens end-to-end.
summary_for_embedding: A standalone ConceptV design-system specimen page demonstrating the surface-zone (tonal zoning) language: a zone registry, a component-role gallery, and a re-skinned Overview dashboard with a spatial flow layer and a live React tweaks panel driving CSS-var knobs.
intention_for_embedding: To show and prove the tonal-zoning system to the eye — depth-as-undertone, gold-reserved-for-Vi, and one-knob re-tuning — by rendering the rules as a registry plus a real applied dashboard with interactive controls.
